Nomination ID: Nominator_2024-10_Colorado Charter School Institute_Animas High School
Reason for nominating:
Animas High School (AHS) distinguishes itself through a commitment to innovative, student-centered, project-based learning, fostering critical thinking and engaged citizenship. 
Advancing Equity:
•	Positive Relationships: AHS’s model prioritizes strong student-staff relationships through low student-staff ratios and core advisory groups that meet weekly. Additionally, students are empowered with decision-making skills, emphasizing advocacy, multiple perspectives, and solution-oriented thinking, while fostering a positive and inclusive culture built on accountability and peer interaction
•	Community Partnerships: The school's location on the Fort Lewis College campus facilitates collaboration, providing students with enriched learning experiences and promoting inclusivity.
Student-Centered Learning:
•	Project-Based Curriculum: AHS emphasizes hands-on, interdisciplinary projects that connect classroom learning to real-world applications, enhancing student engagement and understanding.
•	Experiential Education: Through initiatives like Osprey Blocks, students participate in immersive studies beyond traditional classrooms, fostering deeper connections to the material and personal growth.
By integrating these approaches, Animas High School effectively advances equity and prioritizes student-centered learning, preparing students for success in college and beyond.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Colorado Charter School Institute_Animas High School
Reason for nominating: Animas High School reimagines traditional high school by centering strong relationships, student voice, and authentic project-based learning as the foundation for student success. Through advisory structures, interdisciplinary projects, community partnerships, and immersive experiences like Osprey Blocks, AHS creates learning environments that are engaging, connected to real-world issues, and responsive to student interests. Students develop a sense of purpose and ownership over their learning as they engage in critical thinking, collaboration, and self-advocacy. AHS empowers students by recognizing them as active contributors to their education, preparing them to lead with confidence and curiosity.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2024-10_Colorado Charter School Institute_Kwiyagat Community Academy
Reason for nominating:
Kwiyagat Community Academy (KCA) distinguishes itself through a profound commitment to integrating Nuchiu culture and language into its educational framework, thereby advancing equity and fostering student-centered learning.
Advancing Equity:
•	Cultural Revitalization: KCA emphasizes the revitalization of Nuchiu language and culture, grounding students in a positive self-identity and fostering mutual respect within the community.
•	Community Integration: As the first Colorado charter school located on an Indian reservation, KCA serves the Ute Mountain Ute community, ensuring that education is accessible and relevant to its students' cultural backgrounds.
Student-Centered Learning:
•	Small Class Sizes: The academy maintains small class sizes, allowing for personalized instruction and stronger student-teacher relationships, which cater to individual learning needs.
•	Interdisciplinary, Indigenous, and Project-Based Approach: KCA employs a holistic teaching methodology that integrates indigenous knowledge with modern perspectives, engaging students actively in their learning processes.
•	High Academic Expectations: The school upholds rigorous academic standards, aiming to develop desired character skills, personal wellness, and community involvement among students.
Through these initiatives, Kwiyagat Community Academy effectively promotes equity and student-centered learning, preparing students to be contributing members of both their local and global communities.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Colorado Charter School Institute_Kwiyagat Community Academy
Reason for nominating: Kwiyagat Community Academy (KCA) reimagines the purpose of school by centering Indigenous knowledge, Nuchiu language, and cultural identity within every aspect of teaching and learning. KCA restructures learning around small class sizes, holistic development, and a curriculum that integrates traditional ecological knowledge, community values, and project-based learning. This approach creates an environment where students feel known, grounded, and empowered, and where school becomes a meaningful extension of cultural revitalization and community strength. By honoring students’ identities and expanding their academic and personal capacities, KCA creates a transformative, engaging learning experience.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2024-10_Colorado Charter School Institute_AXIS International Academy
Reason for nominating:
AXIS International Academy distinguishes itself through a holistic educational approach that integrates dual language immersion with a focus on academic excellence, cross-cultural competence, and social-emotional development.
Advancing Equity:
•	Inclusive Language Immersion: Offering instruction in Spanish, French, or Mandarin Chinese alongside English, AXIS ensures that all students, regardless of background, have the opportunity to become bilingual and biliterate, thereby promoting equitable access to language education.
•	Diverse Community Engagement: The academy serves a diverse student body, fostering an inclusive environment that values and respects various cultural backgrounds, which enhances cross-cultural understanding among students.
Student-Centered Learning:
•	50/50 Language Immersion Model: Students receive instruction 50% of the day in English and 50% in their chosen partner language (Spanish, French, or Mandarin), facilitating deep language acquisition and cultural immersion.
•	Whole Child Approach: AXIS implements the Whole Child Initiative, prioritizing six critical tenets: safe, healthy, engaged, supported, challenged, and sustainable. This approach nurtures social-emotional and cognitive skills, fostering a growth mindset and character development.
•	Social-Emotional Learning (SEL): Utilizing the Yale RULER program, the school emphasizes emotional intelligence, helping students recognize and manage their emotions, which is integral to personal and academic success.
Through these strategies, AXIS International Academy effectively advances equity and prioritizes student-centered learning, preparing students to thrive as multilingual, culturally competent individuals in a global society.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Colorado Charter School Institute_AXIS International Academy
Reason for nominating: AXIS International Academy reimagines learning by integrating multilingual immersion, global competence, and whole-child development into a cohesive, student-centered model. Through its 50/50 immersion structure and language options in Spanish, French, and Mandarin, AXIS reframes the purpose of school as a place where students build cross-cultural understanding and cognitive flexibility. The school’s Whole Child Initiative and emphasis on social-emotional learning create an engaging, nurturing environment where students learn to manage emotions, collaborate effectively, and persevere through challenges. AXIS empowers students to become multilingual, globally minded citizens equipped for a rapidly changing world.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2024-10_Colorado Charter School Institute_Colorado International Language Academy
Reason for nominating:
The Colorado International Language Academy (CILA) distinguishes itself through a robust commitment to multilingual education and cultural immersion, fostering a learning environment that promotes both equity and student-centered learning.
Advancing Equity:
•	Inclusive Language Programs: CILA offers language immersion in German, Mandarin Chinese, and Spanish, providing all students, regardless of background, the opportunity to become proficient in a second language. This approach ensures equitable access to language education, preparing students for a globalized world.
•	Cultural Competence: By integrating diverse cultures into the curriculum, CILA cultivates an appreciation for global perspectives, promoting inclusivity and respect for diversity among students.
Student-Centered Learning:
•	Immersive Instruction: Students spend half of each day immersed in their chosen language, learning from native speakers. This method enhances linguistic skills and cultural understanding, catering to individual learning styles and fostering engagement.
•	Holistic Development: CILA emphasizes not only academic excellence but also the intellectual, physical, and character development of each student, ensuring a well-rounded educational experience.
Through these initiatives, CILA effectively advances equity and prioritizes student-centered learning, equipping students with the skills and knowledge necessary to thrive as global citizens.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Colorado Charter School Institute_Colorado International Language Academy
Reason for nominating: Colorado International Language Academy (CILA) reimagines the student experience by placing multilingualism, cultural immersion, and holistic development at the center of schooling. Through daily immersion in German, Mandarin, or Spanish with native-speaking teachers, CILA structures learning in ways that foster deep engagement, curiosity, and cultural awareness. The school’s focus on intellectual, physical, and character development ensures that students grow as confident, well-rounded individuals. By providing equitable access to rigorous language programs and culturally rich experiences, CILA empowers students to thrive academically and personally in an interconnected global environment.

Pan American International High School at Elmhurst takes a comprehensive approach that supports the linguistic, academic, and socio-emotional development of recent immigrant and refugee youth through a blend of language-rich, interdisciplinary, collaborative, and experiential learning.

MET Professional Academy implements the innovative learning model, CAPS (Center for Advanced Professional Studies), which provides real-world, profession-based learning opportunities through collaborations with business and community partners to prepare students for college and career.

Leaders of Tomorrow implements an innovative model, The Arthur Project, which uses clinically focused mentors and the science of relationship-based learning to support students in maintaining their mental health as they navigate the many demands of middle school life.

New Roads is a progressive school that enrolls a diverse population of learners. Students learn by doing and take a rigorous set of courses that are strategically planned to work with young people's developmental stages (e.g. Human Development courses focus on themes that young people are experiencing socially and emotionally in particular grades). Pedagogy is informed by research and brain science and is in fact an approach being codified by researchers. Students learn to have social awareness, a moral compass, and intellectual resourcefulness to lead meaningful lives to invent the future, and to fulfill their responsibilities as democratic citizens in a global community. Many courses and projects focus on promoting democracy, making a difference in the local community, and knowing oneself.
This school features a very strong academic program.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2024-10_Transcend_Power Center Academy Hickory Hill Elementary School
Official School Name: Power Center Academy Hickory Hill Elementary School
Reason for nominating: Power Center Academy Hickory Hill Elementary School implements an innovative model, The Gentlemen’s League, an all-male mentorship program that creates opportunities for boys of color to have positive, joyful, affirming experiences that lead to lives of achievement.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2024-10_The Forest School_The Forest School Online
Reason for nominating: The Forest School Online stands out for its innovative, learner-driven approach that prioritizes student agency, real-world problem-solving, and self-directed learning. By fostering an environment where students take ownership of their education, the school advances equity by meeting learners where they are, providing flexible pathways, and ensuring access to meaningful, personalized learning experiences. Its focus on authentic roles, collaborative challenges, and reflection creates a truly student-centered model that empowers diverse learners to thrive academically, socially, and emotionally.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2024-10_Center on Reinventing Public Education_New England Innovation Academy
Reason for nominating: NEIA upper level school offers cross-disciplinary courses centered on real world, student-led projects. These include several focus areas which are uncommon in the same curriculum: entrepreneurship, design/innovation, and humanities.

Sora began designing an online, project-based high school experience before the pandemic struck. The school's faculty advise student-driven and interdisciplinary projects that map to a competency framework. Sora is an approach worth watching in particular since more inquiry-driven instructional approaches are often thought to require intensive teacher time and supervision, thus not being a good fit for virtual schooling - but Sora's design offers a solution worth learning from.

[**This nomination is from Next Generation Learning Challenges, not Mass IDEAS! Mass IDEAS is no longer active.]

In 2012 Eminence Independent School District ignited its School on FIRE model, from PK to 12. Envisioning the Disney World of schools, the School on FIRE model is designed to develop world-class students and empower them to be risk-takers, collaborators, communicators, leaders, and visionaries. These outcomes are expressed in the Eminence Exemplars, the district’s version of a graduate portrait, which have been built out into grade-level standards across all grades, PK-12. With a STEAM approach, their learning model embraces experiential learning using advanced science, emerging technologies, applied engineering skills, and artistic design. Classroom experiences are designed to capture the heart and imagination of students, driven by the tenets of ‘Surprise & Delight’ and ‘Yes, And’ thinking! The Edhub is a multimedia, tech-enabled, play-based learning space in the school building shared by elementary, middle, and high school students and staff. Students actively engaged in learning by moving, creating, and doing; student work is displayed everywhere; and teachers create meaningful moments of both joy and productive struggle in every grade, every subject, every year. The school’s personalized, passion-based approach is supported by standards-based grading and micro-credentials; panel defenses in 5th, 8th, and 12th grade; seasonal rites of passage; and a design thinking curriculum.

This school features a very strong academic program, project-based learning, career and technical education, and service learning. Brookline High School implements an innovative model, The Calculus Project, which works to accelerate mathematics learning for students furthest from opportunity in order to give them access to higher-level mathematics courses in high school and college.

District or CMO: New York City Department of Education
Reason for nominating: This school partners with New York City schools to offer them a half-day program featuring career and technical education in five diverse pathways.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2024-10_Science Leadership Academy_Science Leadership Academy Middle School
Reason for nominating:
Profound focus on an inquiry-driven, project-based caring approach to education. 
School-wide focus on Grading for Equity.
Four year Advisory program that ensures all students have an advocate and all parents have a first point of contact that is constant over time.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Next Generation Learning Challenges_International Academy at Francis Hammond Middle School
Official School Name: International Academy at Francis Hammond Middle School
Reason for nominating:
The International Academy is a program of academic services within Francis C. Hammond Middle School offered to recently arrived immigrant students who are learning the English language. Students are on a team with teachers who are specially trained to teach students the core academic content and the English language simultaneously. In addition, students have a school counselor and administrator dedicated to meeting their specific needs. Students have access to all elective classes, physical education and health classes, and extracurricular activities and clubs that are offered at Francis C. Hammond Middle School.

Strong adult teaming at the International Academy supports multilingual learners through a coordinated system of instruction, student supports, and tiered interventions. Students engage in project-based learning while collaborating in groups with diverse levels of language proficiency and native languages; this approach accelerates students' learning of academic English while studying a rigorous curriculum.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2024-10_Transcend_International Academy at Francis Hammond Middle School
Official School Name: International Academy at Francis Hammond Middle School
Reason for nominating: International Academy at Francis Hammond Middle School takes a comprehensive approach that supports the linguistic, academic, and socio-emotional development of recent immigrant and refugee youth through a blend of language-rich, interdisciplinary, collaborative, and experiential learning.

Cherry Creek implements the innovative learning model, CAPS (Center for Advanced Professional Studies), which provides real-world, profession-based learning opportunities through collaborations with business and community partners to prepare students for college and career.

The school has implemented mastery-based learning system and uses the Mastery Transcript as the official summative record for some or all graduates or supplements the legacy transcript with the MTC Learning Record reporting on "durable skills) (aka "portrait of a gradudate" competencies).

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Center for Innovation in Education (C!E)_The Match High School and Career Center
Official School Name: The Match High School and Career Center
Reason for nominating: The Match is a newly launched high school from Matchbook Learning, building on the K-8 school's model of personalized learning model in the unique context of a turnaround school. The Match high school is taking personalized learning to the next level by integrating a career readiness. Students participate in career pathways supported by community partners in fields like welding, engineering, computer science, healthcare, media technology, entrepreneurship, and more. Not only are students gaining real-world career experience and getting plugged into the local economy, they are also getting world-class academics. Even in its inaugural year, the Match out-performed peer schools in state standardized assessments and PSAT.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Institute for Personalized Learning_James E. Dottke Project-Based Learning High School
Official School Name: James E. Dottke Project-Based Learning High School
Reason for nominating: Dottke PBL High School transformed from an alternative school where learners were sent because they didn't fit into the traditional school system to a school of choice for learners who want to learn differently. The Dottke community is an inspiration of what learning could and should be in high school. The Dottke team takes pride in that fact that their learning experiences are "different by design" and are really student centered. This learning environment is a hidden gem in the Milwaukee area that everyone should be aware of and visit.

Sussex emphasizes the whole learner in that in addition to academics they have strong programming in outdoor education and the arts. They have daily, weekly, yearly routines that foster strong community and culture. They are continuing to push their own boundaries and assumptions to grow and iterate on their established practices.

Watershed starts the school year with an extended wilderness trip in advisory--where culture and connection are formed. Throughout the school year students are engaged deeply in interdisciplinary projects that often see them off campus working and learning in community. The school year ends with learning on key topics off campus in through extended domestic and international trips.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_NY Charter School Office_"AECI 1 - The NYC Charter School for Architecture, Engineering, and Construction Industries"
Official School Name: "AECI 1 - The NYC Charter School for Architecture, Engineering, and Construction Industries"
Reason for nominating: Theme-related school faculty have worked in their fields and bring the expertise and real-world examples to the students. In addition to the rigorous curriculum and dual credit opportunities, the school has a course for all juniors called “College Readiness” and for seniors called “Post-secondary Readiness.” This speaks to the school’s dedication to the personal growth of their students as well as their academic growth.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_NY Charter School Office_Global Concepts Charter School
Reason for nominating: The school’s mission is to “use an innovative approach to challenge and support its students to have a positive impact on an ever-changing world.” The school offers STEAM instruction for technology education, K-12. Students experience opportunities for college credit, internships, and can explore certificate programs in culinary arts, digital media arts, nursing, business management, and entrepreneurship.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_NY Charter School Office_Health Sciences Charter School
Reason for nominating: The school’s mission is “Health Sciences provide high school age youth with academic learning environments that prepare them to communicate effectively, think and reason critically, value diversity, engage in service learning, pursue academic excellence, and obtain sustainable and quality careers in the health sciences industry.” The school offers opportunities for students to experience dual enrollment and earn college credit; internships in health care and related fields; shadowing; volunteering at healthcare sites; medical guest speakers; community service and 21st century skills. Certificate programs include nursing assistant, physical fitness, Licensed Practical Nurse, phlebotomy, physical technician, and EKG technician.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_NY Charter School Office_Charter School For Applied Technologies
Reason for nominating: This school’s motto is “Every day is career day.” Their mission is “to prepare students to attain family-sustaining careers by integrating career exploration and a lifelong learning culture.” Students experience opportunities for college credit, internships, certificate programs in health care, business, a hands-on greenhouse that functions as a learning lab and community service, and Early College and Career Pathways, including Project Lead the Way in manufacturing.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Indiana Charter Innovation Center_The Portage School Of Leaders
District or CMO: South Bend Career and Success Academies
Reason for nominating: Andrew Hoyt is a visionary leader who has created a school that is a great example of the next horizon of education. Students are integrated into the community during the learning process and are measured against a wide range of competencies and durable skills.

Jeffco Open has been a pillar of innovation the Denver Metro area for decades. Their pedagogy is progressive grounded in student agency and projects. The path to graduation is through a series of independent projects demonstrating learning in key competencies. They foster agency also through student planned trips nationally and internationally.

The STEAD School is a Science, Technology, Environmental, Agricultural and systems Design charter high school focused on solving global food, health, energy, and environmental issues.

STEAD engages students in real life, hands-on learning, utilizing a Career and Technical Education (CTE) Agricultural Pathways curriculum in plant, animal, food, and environmental sciences. STEAD uses a Project Based Learning (PBL) approach integrating CTE curriculum and aligning guiding questions into a PBL framework where students explore these questions and utilize their findings as the foundation for projects. In addition to the scientific pathways of study, lessons in math, art, music, foreign language, English language arts, and history align with projects and engage students in deeply focused studies, Socratic seminar and community presentations.

Gran Via is deeply rooted in community responsiveness, listening to the needs of the students and families they serve and building their program for them. Gran Via has returned learning to nature, they meet each day in Westland Park as a forest school/nature school program. Due to the intentionally small nature of the school, Gran Via knows all of their students well, sees them for who they are, and responds to their unique needs in real time. They are relationally focused, while creating a rich learning environment for every child.

In 2005, the Boston Teachers Union and the Boston School Committee negotiated an agreement under which the Boston Teachers Union would open a new, teacher-run school to showcase their commitment to educating Boston’s children. Both the Union and the Boston Public Schools were excited about this unprecedented partnership, and worked hard to make it a reality. A committee of educators and union officers met regularly during the 2008-09 school year to plan and design the school. The first lead teachers were hired in February 2009, a partnership with Simmons College was formalized later that spring, and the Boston Teachers Union School opened its doors in September 2009, with 150 students in grades K1-2 and grade 6. By September 2011, we were a full K-8 school with approximately 340 students.

We are a shared leadership school. We do not have a principal, but instead direct the operation and development of the school through our committee structure, and our weekly faculty meetings. Our decision making is intentional and many decisions are made by consensus. Two lead teachers with a reduced teaching load perform many of the daily administrative tasks. We are part of a national network of teacher-powered schools.

Duggan Academy is an teacher-powered Expeditionary Learning community dedicated to upholding high academic expectations through authentic learning experiences. Its students, grades 6-12, attain academic success and demonstrate exemplary character.

Duggan Middle School provides two hours per week of embedded enrichment with community partners as well as robust varsity and junior varsity athletic programs.

Duggan High School offers a variety of internships, AP courses, and dual enrollment classes in partnership with Western New England University, Springfield Technical Community College, Springfield College, and Westfield State; it graduated its first class in 2019.

Washtenaw International High School is a public consortium, Teacher-Powered, International Baccalaureate (IB) High School for academically focused students in participating school districts in Washtenaw County, Wayne County, Oakland County, and Livingston County. WIHI opened its doors in the fall of 2011 with approximately 100 students and now serves 490 students in grades 9 through 12.

WIHI provides a rigorous, student-centered, inquiry-based instructional and curricular program with an emphasis on international mindedness, interdisciplinary knowledge, leadership development, and critical thinking.

Washtenaw International High School ranks #1 in Washtenaw County for ACT and College Readiness, and #2 in the state of Michigan. WIHI’s exceptional staff have a wealth of international experience, academic expertise, and passion for teaching and learning.

In addition to earning a high school diploma, all students at WIHI work towards earning an International Baccalaureate Diploma, regarded as the most rigorous and reputable high school curriculum in the world.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Teach for America_Elizabeth City-Pasquotank Public Schools Micro School
Official School Name: Elizabeth City-Pasquotank Public Schools Micro School
Reason for nominating:
Elizabeth City’s micro school is reimagining what school can be inside a traditional public system. Students work in mixed grade communities and spend their days designing a real Airbnb concept, merging science, social studies, and English standards into a meaningful project with authentic audience and purpose. Young people lead the marketing plan, develop the concept, and manage the full creative process, gaining skills in research, writing, collaboration, and design.

The learning environment extends into the community. Students take gym at a local fitness center, study photography with a local photographer, and create art at a local performing arts studio. This approach opens up the entire region as a learning campus and shows what is possible when schools partner deeply with local talent.

Perhaps most importantly, the school is proving that bold innovation can flourish in a traditional district. With a superintendent who completed Transcend’s rural leadership cohort and is committed to big, human centered change, the team has navigated bureaucratic structures to create experiences that are engaging, effective, and empowering for all students.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Getting Smart_Notre Dame de Sion High School for Girls
Official School Name: Notre Dame de Sion High School for Girls
Reason for nominating: As a college-prep school, that's by far not the only thing they prepare these young ladies for. Sion structures the school year by incorporating ample opportunities for the ladies to learn about themselves through interdisciplinary deep dives, cultural immersion events, community service outreach projects and travel abroad experiences. The first week of school is dedicated to a theme each year and allows the students to lead non-academic sessions, grow in leadership skills development, and learn about the school's rich history. The school is truly dedicated to developing the whole student by bringing in the community to lead workshops ranging from self-defense to managing a budget that will support the growth of the ladies for years to come.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Koshkonong Trails School_High Marq Environmental Charter School
Reason for nominating: For 15 years and counting, High Marq has provided a unique educational opportunity for students in rural Marquette County, Wisconsin. With a focus on environmental learning and community service, High Marq staff prepare students for careers and post-secondary options with a flexibility that meets every student's unique needs.

Kind Academy was built in response to the school offerings in the area. They have created an environment where students and families not traditionally exposed to Montessori are thriving while being culturally relevant and embedding SEL into daily practices. KIND Academy offers families hybrid offerings for flexibility and embraces their location in Florida to have immersive learning experiences with nature and aquatic wildlife. Students who were not thriving in prior environments, find the personalized learning and family like structure supportive.

Kalon Prep Academy, located in rural Minnesota, reimagines high school through a strengths-based approach that helps students discover who they are and where they want to go. Using the REACH model—focused on relationships, education, accountability, character, and hard work—the school supports students academically, socially, and emotionally as they prepare for life after graduation. Small class sizes and personalized coaching allow students to build on their strengths, set meaningful goals, and develop essential life skills. With opportunities to explore careers, visit colleges, and engage in real-world learning, Kalon Prep creates a supportive, future-focused environment where students can grow into confident, capable young adults ready for their next steps.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Innovative Quality Schools_E.C.H.O. Charter School (Every Child Has Opportunities)
Official School Name: E.C.H.O. Charter School (Every Child Has Opportunities)
Reason for nominating: E.C.H.O. Charter School, located in rural Minnesota, reimagines learning by blending a close-knit, relationship-driven environment with strong career and technical education opportunities that prepare students for life beyond high school. Its small size and low student-to-teacher ratios ensure that every learner is known, supported, and able to receive individualized attention. The school’s emphasis on hands-on, project-based learning allows students to explore practical skills and real-world career pathways while still in high school. Rooted in its rural community yet forward-looking in its design, E.C.H.O. creates a learning experience that connects academic growth with meaningful, future-ready opportunities for every student.

BlueSky Online stands out for its deeply personalized, whole-child model that surrounds every student with a dedicated team—including an advisor, counselor, social worker, and teachers—who work collaboratively to guide both academic progress and personal growth. Learning is flexible and responsive, with options for structured, teacher-led courses or self-paced pathways that honor each student’s needs and circumstances. BlueSky intentionally develops and assesses learning in broader, more meaningful ways, emphasizing skills like responsible decision-making, self-management, and real-world readiness. By pairing individualized instruction with comprehensive social-emotional support, the school reimagines online education as a relationship-centered, empowering experience that helps students thrive as learners and as whole people.

The Warwick School District is a newly formed district in Franklin County, Massachusetts. After the regional school district closed down the town’s only elementary school in 2020, community members came together to identify innovative ways to keep their children in the community and decided to launch their own school district. The school is a K-8 STEAM/nature-based, project-based learning school that measures student success through community exhibitions of learning and portfolios as well as through standard measures of learning, such as annual growth in ELA in Math. Through a multi-age classroom model, students are exposed to different learners and benefit from deep personalization. The team aims to provide a blueprint for other rural communities looking to collaborate with surrounding towns on innovative ways to build out their own local districts.

Kindle Education is a racially and socioeconomically diverse 6th–12th grade public charter school in Jersey City that empowers students through a student-driven, project-based, and inquiry-oriented model. Our holistic approach combines rigorous academics with real-world application, personalized support, and a deep focus on purpose, individuality, and community.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Center on Reinventing Public Education_The Village School
Reason for nominating: This small school is centered around self-directed learning. Students learn through projects and play, demonstrating growth through flexible proficiency rather than worksheets and assessments. In their own words, at TVS they "focus first on nurturing a sense of safety in our learners– security in who they are, what they are capable of, where they belong, and why they matter."

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Center on Reinventing Public Education_New Hampshire Career Academy
District or CMO: The New Hampshire Charter School Foundation
Reason for nominating: NHCA exists at the intersection of education and workforce development, creating direct pathways from the classroom to the state’s most in-demand careers. Along the way, students gain hands-on experience, mentorship, and professional confidence that prepare them to thrive in whatever comes next, continuing their education, entering the workforce, or both. Through a collaboration with the Community College System of New Hampshire (CCSNH) and partnerships with local employers, students learn on college campuses, earn two degrees, and build the skills that open doors to higher education and meaningful careers.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Center on Reinventing Public Education_Denver Green School Northfield
Reason for nominating: As part of the Luminary Learning Network's innovation zone, this school  uses practices like not having a principal, using non-licensed teachers for electives, giving teachers more freedoms for when they take personal days, more planning minutes, and an increased role for students and educators in school decision making. The school model emphasizes real world experiences through excursions and an emphasis on sustainability.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Center on Reinventing Public Education_Mark Twain Dual Language Academy

Initially authorized for grades 5-8, Forte expanded in 2023 to include a high school program and is now chartered to serve grades 5-12.

Students benefit from an environment that emphasizes character development through core values: service, teamwork, resilience, integrity, voice, and excellence.
Beyond academics, students have opportunities to participate in music and the arts at every grade level, fostering creativity and cultural pride. The high school program builds on these foundations by deepening community identity, developing leadership, accelerating college credit accumulation, and career exploration.
Forte’s commitment to high-quality instruction is evident through a strong focus on curriculum development, carefully crafted lesson plans, and ongoing professional development for teachers. The school also places a strong emphasis on partnerships with families and local organizations, ensuring that the entire community is engaged in student success.
Today, Forte Preparatory stands as a vibrant school community where students are empowered to strive for excellence and lead with integrity. Through a rigorous academic program, a robust arts curriculum, and a focus on character, students graduate prepared not only for college but for lives of purpose and leadership.

Learning at Shields is highly collaborative, cross-disciplinary, and customized. Students participate in extended, interdisciplinary learning blocks that incorporate small-group, project-based, and technology-rich activities across all content areas. They rotate through four stations: teacher-led small-group instruction, adaptive curriculum at a computer station, independent self-directed practice, and a collaboration station for real-world problem-solving with peers. Teachers flexibly adapt these modalities based on student data to ensure appropriate challenge and support. Throughout these experiences, students are held to high expectations with rigorous learning, encouraged to persist through challenges, and are supported to access the resources they need to keep trying. Students also receive targeted interventions in math and reading during Genius Lab every Friday.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_National Charter Schools Institute_West Michigan Aviation Academy
Reason for nominating: WMAA provides an aviation and STEM focused high school experience, located on the campus of the Gerald R. Ford International Airport in Grand Rapids, MI. Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_NewSchools Venture Fund_South Shore Charter School
Reason for nominating: South Shore Charter School is a tuition-free public charter school currently serving K–5 scholars at their flagship campus in Central Islip, NY. Rooted in high expectations, academic excellence, and strong character development, every student experiences inquiry based learning, a challenging and rigorous curriculum, excellent teaching reinforced by daily educator development, academic and social emotional support, and extended learning time. The results speak for themselves. In the first two years of operation, South Shore students were in the 99th percentile of academic growth nationally in ELA and Math on NWEA assessments, and the school re-enrolled 93% of students and retained 93% of staff.

The school reimagines education by making skills explicit, creating genuine experiences with external partners, and fully integrating competency development across all learning. Students transform from tentative freshmen into confident professionals who embrace failure as learning, communicate with diverse stakeholders, and drive meaningful community impact through sustained, purposeful work.

Nomination ID: Nominator_2025-10_Public Impact_Carlsbad Intermediate School: PR Leyva campus
Official School Name: Carlsbad Intermediate School: PR Leyva campus
Reason for nominating:
PR Leyva and its district are part of the national Opportunity Culture initiative, in which schools extend the reach of excellent teachers and their teams to many more students, for more pay, within regular budgets. 
The school has achieved high-growth learning results for its students; it posted the fourth-highest growth in the state in the math proficiency rate among large schools, and the 17th-highest growth in the literacy rate. It received Certified Opportunity Culture® School status, Level 1, signifying a focus on student access to instruction led by Multi-Classroom Leader® teaching teams and incorporation of small-group, high-dosage tutoring into daily schedules. 
The foundation of the Opportunity Culture model is the Multi-Classroom Leader role, an excellent teacher who leads a small teaching team, providing instructional guidance and frequent, on-the-job development, while continuing to teach part of the time. The school uses this and other teacher and paraprofessional team roles to reach more students with excellent teaching.
